# Codex Telegram Bridge v0.2.6

This release hardens the bridge's communication and scheduling foundation across Telegram,
Codex app-server, workload execution, persistence, and service recovery.

## Highlights

- Recovers stale Telegram polling transports per Bot without taking down the whole bridge or
  replaying lifecycle presence messages during ordinary network recovery.
- Adds weighted outbound lanes, bounded retries, durable non-idempotent delivery intents, queue
  caps, generation-aware shutdown, and protection against stale workers replaying requests after a
  restart.
- Isolates delivery, dashboard, control-bot, and discussion-bot workloads so a slow target cannot
  starve unrelated communication.
- Hardens Codex WebSocket/RPC generation handling, notification and server-request backpressure,
  resync, and startup ordering.
- Migrates state storage to schema v10 with compact event receipts, managed-thread timelines,
  exact TUI approval facts, bounded retention, and read-only JSON status output.
- Adds persistent health snapshots covering polling, outbound, delivery, workload, Codex, resync,
  and database state.
- Throttles systemd restart storms with a five-minute start limit and a 30-second restart delay.

## Upgrade notes

- The state database migrates automatically to schema v10 and creates a private SQLite backup
  before migration. Existing v8 and v9 state is supported.
- Existing credentials, Telegram bindings, Codex state, session spaces, and installer-owned units
  are preserved by the v0.2.6 installer.
- The supported installer host remains Ubuntu or Debian under WSL2 with systemd and Python 3.14.
